subject: Databases
title: Subqueries
description: A short lesson to help you apply Subqueries.

hook.text: Find users who spent more than average. First calculate the average, then filter. A subquery does both.

buildup.text: WHERE total > (SELECT AVG(total) FROM orders). The inner query runs first, then the outer uses its result.

discovery.text: Subqueries work in SELECT, FROM, and WHERE. In FROM, they create a temporary table for the outer query.

twist.text: Correlated subqueries reference the outer query. They rerun for every row. That can be very slow.

climax.text: Often a JOIN can replace a subquery with better performance. But subqueries are more readable for some logic.

punchline.text: Subqueries nest logic. Use them when joins feel forced.

quiz.question: Why are correlated subqueries potentially slow?
options: They re-execute for every row in the outer query
They can only return one column
They bypass the query planner entirely
